ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT ADVANCE MECHANICAL CONTRACTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT ADVANCE MECHANICAL CONTRACTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF AND PARAGRAPH 8 OF THE DECLARATION OF MR. AY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO IRRESPIRABLE FIBERS FROM ASBESTOS-CONTAINING TRANSITE PIPE DISTURBED BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DID NOT CONTAIN ANY PURPORTED UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S EXPOSURE TO ANY ASBESTOS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING OR CONTRIBUTING TO PLAINTIFF'S ASSERTED ASBESTOS-RELATED ILLNESS, BUT EVEN IF IT HAD DONE SO AND DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION ON THAT ISSUE, THE DECLARATION OF DR. BRUCH CREATED A TRIABLE ISSUE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AND PARAGRAPH 8 OF MR. AY'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ALLEGED EXPOSURE BASED ON HIS USE OF RAYLOC BRAKES. BY FAILING TO ASK PLAINTIFF ABOUT RAYLOC BRAKES AT SESSIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION THAT TOOK PLACE SUBSEQUENT TO SERVICE OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES, DEFENDANT DID NOT SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. (SCHEIDING). EVEN IF DEFENDANT DID SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AND MR. LECOUR'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING RAYLOC BRAKES ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AND MR. LECOUR'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Randall K. Bernard, Esq., of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Pneumo Abex LLC
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
/ opposing party James Feduska

RULING:
DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's supplemental interrogatory responses alleged exposure based on his use of Rayloc brakes. By failing to ask plaintiff about Rayloc brakes at sessions of plaintiff's deposition that took place subsequent to service of the supplemental responses, defendant did not sustain its burden of production. (Scheiding). Even if defendant did sustain its burden of production, plaintiff's declaration and Mr. LeCour's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Rayloc brakes attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and Mr. LeCour's deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer M. Ways, Esq., of PRINDLE, AMARO, GOETZ, HILLYARD, BARNES & REINHOLTZ, LLP (415-788-8354), appearing for defendant / moving party Advance Mechanical Contractors, Inc.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Feduska

RULING:
DEFENDANT ADVANCE MECHANICAL CONTRACTORS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Advance Mechanical Contractors, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of plaintiff and paragraph 8 of the declaration of Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to irrespirable fibers from asbestos-containing transite pipe disturbed by defendant. Defendant's separate statement did not contain any purported undisputed material facts on the issue of whether plaintiff's exposure to any asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's asserted asbestos-related illness, but even if it had done so and defendant satisfied its burden of production on that issue, the declaration of Dr. Bruch created a triable issue. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and paragraph 8 of Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
